questions.question: What is a wavefront?
options: A locus of points having the same phase of oscillation
The path light travels in a vacuum
questions.explanation: A wavefront is defined as a surface of constant phase.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: What shape is the wavefront from a point source in an isotropic medium?
questions.explanation: For a point source emitting waves in all directions, the locus of points oscillating in phase is a sphere, creating a spherical wavefront.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: According to Huygens, each point on a wavefront acts as a:
questions.explanation: Huygens' principle states that each point of a wavefront is the source of a secondary disturbance.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: At a very large distance from a source, a small portion of a spherical wavefront can be considered a:
questions.explanation: Over a small area at a large distance from the source, the wavefront appears as a plane.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: The speed of secondary wavelets in a medium is ________ the speed of the wave itself.
questions.explanation: Secondary wavelets spread out in all directions with the speed of the wave.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: Huygens' principle was initially criticized because it could not explain why:
questions.explanation: Huygens' model originally faced a 'logical deficiency' regarding the absence of a backwave.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: Which physicist is credited with the quote, 'God said, Let Newton be! and all was light'?
questions.explanation: The chapter opens with this quote by Alexander Pope.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: When a plane wave undergoes refraction into a denser medium, its speed:
questions.explanation: If a medium is optically denser, the speed of the wave (v) is less than the speed in the rarer medium (c).
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: The refractive index 'n' is defined as the ratio of:
questions.explanation: Refractive index n12 is defined as the ratio of speed in medium 1 to speed in medium 2.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: Which phenomenon was used by Thomas Young to demonstrate the wave nature of light?
questions.explanation: Thomas Young made the first demonstration of interference in 1801.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: For 'Constructive Interference' to occur, the phase difference between two waves must be:
questions.explanation: Constructive interference occurs when the phase difference is an even multiple of π (0, ±2π, etc.).
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: In 'Destructive Interference', the intensity of the resulting wave is:
questions.explanation: Destructive interference occurs where the phase difference is an odd multiple of π, leading to minimum intensity.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: Two sources are called 'coherent' if:
options: They emit light of the same frequency and a constant phase difference
They are placed very close to each other
questions.explanation: Coherent sources must maintain a constant phase difference over time.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: In Young’s double-slit experiment, the 'fringe width' (β) is given by:
questions.explanation: Fringe width is equal to the distance between two consecutive bright/dark fringes, given by Dλ/d.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: What happens to the fringe width if the distance between the slits 'd' is increased?
questions.explanation: Since fringe width is inversely proportional to 'd', increasing 'd' will decrease the width.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: The phenomenon of bending of light around the corners of an obstacle or aperture is called:
questions.explanation: Diffraction is the general name given to the bending of light around obstacles.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: In a single-slit diffraction experiment, the central maximum is ________ as wide as the other maxima.
questions.explanation: The width of the central maximum is double the width of subsequent maxima.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: Diffraction is significant only when the size of the obstacle is:
options: Comparable to the wavelength of light
questions.explanation: For diffraction to be clearly observable, the size of the opening/obstacle must be comparable to the wavelength.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: The resolving power of an optical instrument is limited by:
questions.explanation: Diffraction sets the ultimate limit on the resolving power of microscopes and telescopes.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: The 'Rayleigh Criterion' is used to define the:
questions.explanation: The Rayleigh criterion determines when two close objects can just be distinguished.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: The phenomenon that proves light is a 'transverse' wave is:
questions.explanation: Only transverse waves can be polarized; longitudinal waves cannot.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: A light wave in which the electric field oscillations are restricted to a single plane is called:
questions.explanation: When vibrations are restricted to one plane, the wave is said to be plane polarized or linearly polarized.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: According to Malus' Law, the intensity of light passing through a polarizer (I) is proportional to:
questions.explanation: Intensity varies as the square of the cosine of the angle between the polarizer and analyzer.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: At the 'Brewster angle' (polarizing angle), the reflected light is:
questions.explanation: At the Brewster angle, the reflected light is completely polarized perpendicular to the plane of incidence.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: Which of these devices can be used to produce polarized light?
questions.explanation: A Polaroid is a material used to produce and analyze polarized light.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: What happens to the frequency of light when it passes from air to glass?
questions.explanation: The frequency is a characteristic of the source and does not change during refraction.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: In the 'Doppler Effect' for light, an increase in frequency is called a:
questions.explanation: When light frequency increases (source moving towards observer), it is called a blue shift.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: The 'Red Shift' in the spectrum of distant galaxies indicates that:
questions.explanation: A red shift indicates the source is moving away, providing evidence for the expansion of the universe.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: Fresnel distance (ZF) is the distance beyond which:
options: Divergence due to diffraction becomes significant
questions.explanation: The Fresnel distance marks where the spread due to diffraction equals the size of the aperture.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: In a Young's experiment, if the two slits are covered by unpolarized filters, the interference pattern:
options: Depends on the orientation of the filters
questions.explanation: If the waves from the two slits are polarized in perpendicular directions, they will not interfere.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: A wavefront always moves in the direction ________ to the surface.
questions.explanation: A ray is a line perpendicular to the wavefront, indicating the direction of energy flow.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: The principle of 'Superposition' states that the net displacement is the ________ of individual displacements.
questions.explanation: The resultant displacement is the algebraic (vector) sum of the displacements of individual waves.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: What is the intensity ratio if the amplitude ratio of two interfering waves is 2:1?
questions.explanation: Intensity is proportional to the square of the amplitude.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: If the source of light is a 'linear slit', the wavefront will be:
questions.explanation: A line source generates a cylindrical wavefront.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: Polarization by 'Reflection' was first discovered by:
questions.explanation: Malus discovered the phenomenon of polarization by reflection in 1808.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: In Young's experiment, the central fringe is always:
questions.explanation: At the center, the path difference is zero, leading to constructive interference (bright fringe).
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: The phenomenon of 'Scattering' of light can also produce:
questions.explanation: Light from a clear blue portion of the sky is found to be partially polarized due to scattering.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: A wavefront incident on a thin convex lens becomes ________ after passing through.
questions.explanation: A plane wavefront incident on a convex lens emerges as a spherical wavefront converging to the focus.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: Brewster's Law relates the refractive index 'n' to the polarizing angle 'ip' as:
questions.explanation: The relationship is n = tan ip.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: In a single-slit diffraction, the first minimum occurs when the path difference is:
questions.explanation: The first minimum is found where the path difference sin θ ≈ θ = λ/a.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: Optical resolution of a telescope improves as the aperture diameter 'D':
questions.explanation: A larger aperture reduces the size of the diffraction spot, improving resolution.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: The 'angular width' of the central maximum in diffraction is:
questions.explanation: The central maximum extends from -λ/a to +λ/a, giving a total width of 2λ/a.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: Christian Huygens was a contemporary of which other famous scientist?
questions.explanation: Huygens was a contemporary of Isaac Newton.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: The time taken by light to travel from a source to a wavefront is ________ for all points on the wavefront.
questions.explanation: A wavefront is a surface of constant phase, which corresponds to constant travel time from the source.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: In polarized light, the intensity becomes ________ when the analyzer is perpendicular to the polarizer.
questions.explanation: Using Malus' law, when θ = 90°, cos² θ = 0.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: Young's experiment showed that waves can pass through each other ________.
questions.explanation: The waves maintain their individual characteristics while overlapping.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: If monochromatic light is replaced by white light in Young’s experiment, the central fringe is:
questions.explanation: Since all wavelengths interfere constructively at the center, the fringe remains white.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: The intensity of the first secondary maximum in diffraction is ________ than the central maximum.
questions.explanation: The secondary maxima are significantly weaker than the central maximum.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: Sunlight is considered:
questions.explanation: Sunlight consists of waves with electric fields vibrating in all possible directions.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: The wave theory of light was successful in explaining:
questions.explanation: Wave theory accounts for reflection, refraction, interference, and diffraction.
questions.subject: Physics
